title: Analysis result lookup row — content-keyed FK to a layer envelope
description: One row per (analysis × sample-set × input-artifacts × params) tuple actually computed. The PRIMARY KEY is the content hash of those four components — so two different naming routes that resolve to the same inputs hit the same row. Acts as a unique-index over layers.registry.json: 'has anyone already run analysis A on sample set S against artifacts I with params P?'. The row points at the layer_id of the layer_envelope that holds the actual output. Per REGISTRY_LOOKUP.md §'Content-keyed result lookup'.

result_id.description: Content-derived id. Convention: 'res_' + first 16 hex chars of sha256(analysis_id + '|' + sample_set_id + '|' + sorted(input_artifact_ids).join(',') + '|' + params_hash). Deterministic. PRIMARY KEY of the result-lookup table.

analysis_id.description: Stable tag for the analysis kind (e.g. 'ngsrelate', 'ngspedigree', 'mendelian', 'fst_pairwise', 'theta_pi'). The atlas dispatcher uses analysis_id + the action_manifest.type 1:1 by convention.

analysis_version.description: Optional version tag for the analysis itself (e.g. 'v0.4.1' for ngsRelate). Bumping this invalidates older results without changing the analysis_id.

sample_set_id.description: FK into sample_set_v1.sample_set_id. The CONTENT-derived sample set this result was computed against. Two analyses with the same sample_set_id ran on the exact same members.

input_artifact_ids.description: FKs into layers.registry — the input layers this analysis consumed (e.g. beagle_file_id, sites_file_id, ancestry_q_layer_id). Order does NOT matter for hashing; the engine sorts before hashing. An empty array is allowed for analyses with no recorded artifact inputs (pure-config analyses).

params_hash.description: sha256 of the canonical JSON of all analysis-specific parameters (sorted keys, no whitespace). Bumping a parameter (e.g. -F flag in ngsRelate) produces a new result_id.

params.description: The actual params, kept inline for debugging. Source of truth for params_hash.

output_layer_id.description: FK into layers.registry.json — the layer_envelope this result lives in. The envelope's payload contains the actual tool output.

coordinate_scope.description: Optional coordinate scope of the analysis (per-chrom, whole-genome, custom region). Recorded for convenience filtering. Null = whole-genome / non-coordinate.

status.description: 'active' = canonical. 'stale' = an input layer changed (integrity check). 'superseded' = a newer analysis_version produced a replacement. 'failed' = retained for debugging; output_layer_id may point at an error envelope.

created_by_action_id.description: FK into actions.log.jsonl — the action manifest that produced this result. Null for results registered by backfill (no action_log entry).

_examples._description: ngsRelate result for a single karyotype group on a per-chrom beagle.
Same analysis as above but with the intersection of HOM_INV ∩ ancestry_K8_cluster3. Different sample_set_id, different result_id — but same input artifacts and params.
